verses.sanskrit: श्रीराजोवाचभुयोऽहं श्रोतुमिच्छामि रामस्याद्भ‍ुतकर्मण: ।अनन्तस्याप्रमेयस्य यदन्यत् कृतवान् प्रभु: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-rājovācabhuyo ’haṁ śrotum icchāmirāmasyādbhuta-karmaṇaḥanantasyāprameyasyayad anyat kṛtavān prabhuḥ
verses.text: The glorious King Parīkṣit said: I wish to hear further about Śrī Balarāma, the unlimited and immeasurable Supreme Lord, whose activities are all astounding. What else did He do?

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचनरकस्य सखा कश्चिद् द्विविदो नाम वानर: ।सुग्रीवसचिव: सोऽथ भ्राता मैन्दस्य वीर्यवान् ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācanarakasya sakhā kaściddvivido nāma vānaraḥsugrīva-sacivaḥ so ’thabhrātā maindasya vīryavān
verses.text: Śrī Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: There was an ape named Dvivida who was a friend of Narakāsura’s. This powerful Dvivida, the brother of Mainda, had been instructed by King Sugrīva.

verses.sanskrit: सख्यु: सोऽपचितिं कुर्वन् वानरो राष्ट्रविप्लवम् ।पुरग्रामाकरान् घोषानदहद् वह्निमुत्सृजन् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sakhyuḥ so ’pacitiṁ kurvanvānaro rāṣṭra-viplavampura-grāmākarān ghoṣānadahad vahnim utsṛjan
verses.text: To avenge the death of his friend [Naraka], the ape Dvivida ravaged the land, setting fires that burned cities, villages, mines and cowherd dwellings.

verses.sanskrit: क्व‍‍चित्स शैलानुत्पाट्य तैर्देशान् समचूर्णयत् ।आनर्तान् सुतरामेव यत्रास्ते मित्रहा हरि: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: kvacit sa śailān utpāṭyatair deśān samacūrṇayatānartān sutarām evayatrāste mitra-hā hariḥ
verses.text: Once Dvivida tore up a number of mountains and used them to devastate all the neighboring kingdoms, especially the province of Ānarta, wherein dwelt his friend’s killer, Lord Hari.

verses.sanskrit: क्व‍‍चित् समुद्रमध्यस्थो दोर्भ्यामुत्क्षिप्य तज्जलम् ।देशान् नागायुतप्राणो वेलाकूले न्यमज्जयत् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: kvacit samudra-madhya-sthodorbhyām utkṣipya taj-jalamdeśān nāgāyuta-prāṇovelā-kūle nyamajjayat
verses.text: Another time he entered the ocean and, with the strength of ten thousand elephants, churned up its water with his arms and thus submerged the coastal regions.

verses.sanskrit: आश्रमानृषिमुख्यानां कृत्वा भग्नवनस्पतीन् ।अदूषयच्छकृन्मूत्रैरग्नीन् वैतानिकान् खल: ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: āśramān ṛṣi-mukhyānāṁkṛtvā bhagna-vanaspatīnadūṣayac chakṛn-mūtrairagnīn vaitānikān khalaḥ
verses.text: The wicked ape tore down the trees in the hermitages of exalted sages and contaminated their sacrificial fires with his feces and urine.

verses.sanskrit: पुरुषान् योषितो द‍ृप्त: क्ष्माभृद्‍द्रोणीगुहासु स: ।निक्षिप्य चाप्यधाच्छैलै: पेशष्कारीव कीटकम् ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: puruṣān yoṣito dṛptaḥkṣmābhṛd-dronī-guhāsu saḥnikṣipya cāpyadhāc chailaiḥpeśaṣkārīva kīṭakam
verses.text: Just as a wasp imprisons smaller insects, he arrogantly threw both men and women into caves in a mountain valley and sealed the caves shut with boulders.

verses.sanskrit: एवं देशान् विप्रकुर्वन् दूषयंश्च कुलस्‍त्रिय: ।श्रुत्वा सुललितं गीतं गिरिं रैवतकं ययौ ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ deśān viprakurvandūṣayaṁś ca kula-striyaḥśrutvā su-lalitaṁ gītaṁgiriṁ raivatakaṁ yayau
verses.text: Once, while Dvivida was thus engaged in harassing the neighboring kingdoms and polluting women of respectable families, he heard very sweet singing coming from Raivataka Mountain. So he went there.

verses.sanskrit: तत्रापश्यद् यदुपतिं रामं पुष्करमालिनम् ।सुदर्शनीयसर्वाङ्गं ललनायूथमध्यगम् ॥ ९ ॥गायन्तं वारुणीं पीत्वा मदविह्वललोचनम् ।विभ्राजमानं वपुषा प्रभिन्नमिव वारणम् ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: tatrāpaśyad yadu-patiṁrāmaṁ puṣkara-mālinamsudarśanīya-sarvāṅgaṁlalanā-yūtha-madhya-gam
verses.text: There he saw Śrī Balarāma, the Lord of the Yadus, adorned with a garland of lotuses and appearing most attractive in every limb. He was singing amidst a crowd of young women, and since He had drunk vāruṇī liquor, His eyes rolled as if He were intoxicated. His body shone brilliantly as He behaved like an elephant in rut.

verses.sanskrit: दुष्ट: शाखामृग: शाखामारूढ: कम्पयन् द्रुमान् ।चक्रे किलकिलाशब्दमात्मानं सम्प्रदर्शयन् ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: duṣṭaḥ śākhā-mṛgaḥ śākhāmārūḍhaḥ kampayan drumāncakre kilakilā-śabdamātmānaṁ sampradarśayan
verses.text: The mischievous ape climbed a tree branch and then revealed his presence by shaking the trees and making the sound kilakilā.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य धार्ष्‍ट्यं कपेर्वीक्ष्य तरुण्यो जातिचापला: ।हास्यप्रिया विजहसुर्बलदेवपरिग्रहा: ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasya dhārṣṭyaṁ kaper vīkṣyataruṇyo jāti-cāpalāḥhāsya-priyā vijahasurbaladeva-parigrahāḥ
verses.text: When Lord Baladeva’s consorts saw the ape’s impudence, they began to laugh. They were, after all, young girls who were fond of joking and prone to silliness.

verses.sanskrit: ता हेलयामास कपिर्भ्रूक्षेपैर्सम्मुखादिभि: ।दर्शयन् स्वगुदं तासां रामस्य च निरीक्षित: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tā helayām āsa kapirbhrū-kṣepair sammukhādibhiḥdarśayan sva-gudaṁ tāsāṁrāmasya ca nirīkṣitaḥ
verses.text: Even as Lord Balarāma looked on, Dvivida insulted the girls by making odd gestures with his eyebrows, coming right in front of them, and showing them his anus.

verses.sanskrit: तं ग्राव्णा प्राहरत् क्रुद्धो बल: प्रहरतां वर: ।स वञ्चयित्वा ग्रावाणं मदिराकलशं कपि: ॥ १४ ॥गृहीत्वा हेलयामास धूर्तस्तं कोपयन् हसन् ।निर्भिद्य कलशं दुष्टो वासांस्यास्फालयद् बलम् ।कदर्थीकृत्य बलवान् विप्रचक्रे मदोद्धत: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ grāvṇā prāharat kruddhobalaḥ praharatāṁ varaḥsa vañcayitvā grāvāṇaṁmadirā-kalaśaṁ kapiḥ
verses.text: Angered, Lord Balarāma, the best of fighters, hurled a rock at him, but the cunning ape dodged the rock and grabbed the Lord’s pot of liquor. Further infuriating Lord Balarāma by laughing and by ridiculing Him, wicked Dvivida then broke the pot and offended the Lord even more by pulling at the girls’ clothing. Thus the powerful ape, puffed up with false pride, continued to insult Śrī Balarāma.

verses.sanskrit: तं तस्याविनयं द‍ृष्ट्वा देशांश्च तदुपद्रुतान् ।क्रुद्धो मुषलमादत्त हलं चारिजिघांसया ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ tasyāvinayaṁ dṛṣṭvādeśāṁś ca tad-upadrutānkruddho muṣalam ādattahalaṁ cāri-jighāṁsayā
verses.text: Lord Balarāma saw the ape’s rude behavior and thought of the disruptions he had created in the surrounding kingdoms. Thus the Lord angrily took up His club and His plow weapon, having decided to put His enemy to death.

verses.sanskrit: द्विविदोऽपि महावीर्य: शालमुद्यम्य पाणिना ।अभ्येत्य तरसा तेन बलं मूर्धन्यताडयत् ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: dvivido ’pi mahā-vīryaḥśālam udyamya pāṇināabhyetya tarasā tenabalaṁ mūrdhany atāḍayat
verses.text: Mighty Dvivida also came forward to do battle. Uprooting a śāla tree with one hand, he rushed toward Balarāma and struck Him on the head with the tree trunk.

verses.sanskrit: तं तु सङ्कर्षणो मूर्ध्‍नि पतन्तमचलो यथा ।प्रतिजग्राह बलवान् सुनन्देनाहनच्च तम् ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ tu saṅkarṣaṇo mūrdhnipatantam acalo yathāpratijagrāha balavānsunandenāhanac ca tam
verses.text: But Lord Saṅkarṣaṇa remained as motionless as a mountain and simply grabbed the log as it fell upon His head. He then struck Dvivida with His club, named Sunanda.

verses.sanskrit: मूषलाहतमस्तिष्को विरेजे रक्तधारया ।गिरिर्यथा गैरिकया प्रहारं नानुचिन्तयन् ॥ १९ ॥पुनरन्यं समुत्क्षिप्य कृत्वा निष्पत्रमोजसा ।तेनाहनत् सुसङ्‌क्रु‌द्धस्तं बल: शतधाच्छिनत् ॥ २० ॥ततोऽन्येन रुषा जघ्ने तं चापि शतधाच्छिनत् ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: mūṣalāhata-mastiṣkovireje rakta-dhārayāgirir yathā gairikayāprahāraṁ nānucintayan
verses.text: Struck on the skull by the Lord’s club, Dvivida became brilliantly decorated by the outpour of blood, like a mountain beautified by red oxide. Ignoring the wound, Dvivida uprooted another tree, stripped it of leaves by brute force and struck the Lord again. Now enraged, Lord Balarāma shattered the tree into hundreds of pieces, upon which Dvivida grabbed yet another tree and furiously hit the Lord again. This tree, too, the Lord smashed into hundreds of pieces.

verses.sanskrit: एवं युध्यन् भगवता भग्ने भग्ने पुन: पुन: ।आकृष्य सर्वतो वृक्षान् निर्वृक्षमकरोद् वनम् ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ yudhyan bhagavatābhagne bhagne punaḥ punaḥākṛṣya sarvato vṛkṣānnirvṛkṣam akarod vanam
verses.text: Thus fighting the Lord, who again and again demolished the trees He was attacked with, Dvivida kept on uprooting trees from all sides until the forest was left treeless.

verses.sanskrit: ततोऽमुञ्चच्छिलावर्षं बलस्योपर्यमर्षित: ।तत्सर्वं चूर्णयामास लीलया मुषलायुध: ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tato ’muñcac chilā-varṣaṁbalasyopary amarṣitaḥtat sarvaṁ cūrṇayāṁ āsalīlayā muṣalāyudhaḥ
verses.text: The angry ape then released a rain of stones upon Lord Balarāma, but the wielder of the club easily pulverized them all.

verses.sanskrit: स बाहू तालसङ्काशौ मुष्टीकृत्य कपीश्वर: ।आसाद्य रोहिणीपुत्रं ताभ्यां वक्षस्यरूरुजत् ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa bāhū tāla-saṅkāśaumuṣṭī-kṛtya kapīśvaraḥāsādya rohiṇī-putraṁtābhyāṁ vakṣasy arūrujat
verses.text: Dvivida, the most powerful of apes, now clenched his fists at the end of his palm-tree-sized arms, came before Lord Balarāma and beat his fists against the Lord’s body.

verses.sanskrit: यादवेन्द्रोऽपि तं दोर्भ्यां त्यक्त्वा मुषललाङ्गले ।जत्रावभ्यर्दयत्क्रुद्ध: सोऽपतद् रुधिरं वमन् ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: yādavendro ’pi taṁ dorbhyāṁtyaktvā muṣala-lāṅgalejatrāv abhyardayat kruddhaḥso ’patad rudhiraṁ vaman
verses.text: The furious Lord of the Yādavas then threw aside His club and plow and with His bare hands hammered a blow upon Dvivida’s collarbone. The ape collapsed, vomiting blood.

verses.sanskrit: चकम्पे तेन पतता सटङ्क: सवनस्पति: ।पर्वत: कुरुशार्दूल वायुना नौरिवाम्भसि ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: cakampe tena patatāsa-ṭaṅkaḥ sa-vanaspatiḥparvataḥ kuru-śārdūlavāyunā naur ivāmbhasi
verses.text: When he fell, O tiger among the Kurus, Raivataka Mountain shook, along with its cliffs and trees, like a wind-tossed boat at sea.

verses.sanskrit: जयशब्दो नम:शब्द: साधु साध्विति चाम्बरे ।सुरसिद्धमुनीन्द्राणामासीत् कुसुमवर्षिणाम् ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: jaya-śabdo namaḥ-śabdaḥsādhu sādhv iti cāmbaresura-siddha-munīndrāṇāmāsīt kusuma-varṣiṇām
verses.text: In the heavens the demigods, perfect mystics and great sages cried out, “Victory to You! Obeisances to You! Excellent! Well done!” and showered flowers upon the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: एवं निहत्य द्विविदं जगद्‍व्य‌‍‍तिकरावहम् ।संस्तूयमानो भगवान् जनै: स्वपुरमाविशत् ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ nihatya dvividaṁjagad-vyatikarāvahamsaṁstūyamāno bhagavānjanaiḥ sva-puram āviśat
verses.text: Having thus killed Dvivida, who had disturbed the whole world, the Supreme Lord returned to His capital as the people along the way chanted His glories.
